subtitle: When the Indus Changed Masters and Elephants Shaped Empires

panels.shot: epic low-angle wide of a royal camp beside a broad river, distant ranks of elephants fading into the morning mist
panels.action: Chandragupta Maurya stands tall on a rise above the Indus plain, cloak stirring in the wind, as a vast line of war elephants and soldiers stretches toward the horizon under a pale dawn sky.

dialogue.text: Chandragupta Maurya, lord of Pataliputra, meets Seleucus Nicator on the Indus frontier, where elephants, empires and the fate of the Hindu Kush are weighed in treaty instead of endless war.

i18n.hi: पाटलिपुत्र के स्वामी चंद्रगुप्त मौर्य सिंधु सीमा पर सेल्युकस निकेटर से मिलते हैं, जहाँ हाथी, साम्राज्य और हिन्दूकुश की किस्मत जंग नहीं, संधि में तोली जाती है।

panels.shot: broad bird's-eye over a stylized map-like landscape from the Aegean to the Indus
panels.action: A sweeping vista shows rugged mountains, the winding Indus, and distant cities marked by Greek columns in the west and Indian stupas and palace roofs in the east, divided by the high wall of the Hindu Kush.

dialogue.text: Nearly twenty years after Alexander crossed the Indus, his empire lay broken, carved into Hellenistic kingdoms, while in the east a new Indian power rose fast along the same roads of conquest.

i18n.hi: अलेक्ज़ांडर के सिंधु पार करने के करीब बीस साल बाद उसका साम्राज्य टूट चुका था, यूनानी रियासतों में बँट गया था, और पूर्व में वही विजयी रास्तों पर नई भारतीय शक्ति तेज़ी से उठ रही थी।

panels.shot: medium shot inside a Mauryan war tent, lit by oil lamps
panels.action: Chandragupta Maurya leans over a palm-leaf map weighted with stones, pointing toward the northwest passes as advisors stand in respectful silence around him.

dialogue.text: From Pataliputra on the Ganga, Chandragupta had driven Nanda power from Magadha, broken Macedonian garrisons in the northwest, and pressed his lion seal into the borderlands of Gandhara and Arachosia.

i18n.hi: गंगा किनारे पाटलिपुत्र से चंद्रगुप्त ने नंदों को मगध से उखाड़ा, उत्तर-पश्चिम की मकदूनी छावनियाँ तोड़ीं और अपने सिंह-मुद्रांक गांधार–अराख़ोशिया की सरहदों पर जड़ दिए।

panels.shot: dramatic low-angle of a Greek-style statue of Seleucus against a stormy sky
panels.action: A stone or bronze likeness of Seleucus Nicator in cuirass and cloak stands by a city wall with Greek battlements, clouds gathering behind him like a rising storm.

dialogue.text: In Babylon, Seleucus Nicator rebuilt his strength from Alexander's wreckage, winning Mesopotamia and Iran, and now he turned his gaze east, toward the satrapies beyond the Hindu Kush.

i18n.hi: बाबिलोन में सेल्युकस निकेटर ने अलेक्ज़ांडर की विरासत से अपनी ताकत फिर जोड़ी, मेसोपोटामिया और ईरान जीते और अब उसकी नज़रें हिन्दूकुश के पार पूर्वी सूबों पर टिक गईं।

panels.shot: wide tracking shot of a Seleucid column moving through a high mountain pass
panels.action: Greek and eastern soldiers march in ordered ranks through a narrow, snow-dusted pass, their bronze helmets and long sarissas catching the thin mountain light, cavalry and baggage trailing behind.

dialogue.text: Appian tells of Seleucus marching once more over the mountains toward India, seeking to reclaim Alexander's eastern satrapies and to push his frontiers again to the Indus.

i18n.hi: एपियन लिखता है कि सेल्युकस फिर पहाड़ पार कर भारत की ओर चला, अलेक्ज़ांडर के पूर्वी सूबे वापस लेने और अपनी सरहदें फिर से सिंधु तक धकेलने को।

panels.shot: wide shot on a dusty plain where Indian and Greek forces face each other at a distance
panels.action: Across a broad plain framed by low hills, a Mauryan army with elephants and chariots stands in disciplined lines facing a distant Macedonian-style phalanx with banners fluttering in the dry wind.

dialogue.text: On the western marches, near the old routes of Gandhara and the Paropamisadae, Indian and Macedonian formations once again faced each other, as they had in Alexander's day.

i18n.hi: पश्चिमी सरहद पर, गांधार और पेरोपामिसादाई के पुराने रास्तों के पास, भारतीय और मकदूनी सेनाएँ फिर आमने-सामने थीं, जैसे अलेक्ज़ांडर के दिनों में।

panels.shot: medium close-up of Chandragupta atop a war elephant, surveying the field
panels.action: Chandragupta, armored and composed, sits on a howdah atop a restless elephant, watching the distant foreign phalanx with a steady gaze as attendants hold his spear and parasol.

dialogue.text: This time the eastern king was no local raja. Chandragupta commanded a vast host, hardened from earlier campaigns, and wielded the heavy beasts that Alexander had both feared and used.

i18n.hi: इस बार पूरब का राजा कोई स्थानीय राजक नहीं, चंद्रगुप्त विशाल, तपे-तपाए लश्कर का सेनापति था, और उन्हीं भारी जानवरों को साध रहा था जिनसे अलेक्ज़ांडर डरता भी था और काम भी लेता था।

panels.shot: medium wide of Seleucid officers in council inside a campaign pavilion
panels.action: Seleucid generals gather around a wooden table strewn with parchments, one pointing anxiously toward markers shaped like elephants set on the board, while a stern figure listens in thought.

dialogue.text: The Greek sources do not linger on each clash, but their silence is heavy; Seleucus, no fool, must have measured his stretched lines against the depth of Mauryan power and the long road back to Syria.

i18n.hi: यूनानी लेखक हर भिड़ंत पर नहीं ठहरते, पर उनकी ख़ामोशी बहुत कुछ कहती है; बेवकूफ नहीं, सेल्युकस ने जरूर अपनी खिंची पंक्तियों की तुलना मौर्य शक्ति की गहराई और सीरिया तक की लंबी वापसी से की होगी।

panels.shot: intimate medium shot of Chandragupta with a trusted counselor at dusk
panels.action: In the dim glow of a small campfire, Chandragupta sits on a low stool as a counselor gestures toward the west, the silhouettes of elephants looming in the background against the fading light.

dialogue.text: Chandragupta too had much to weigh: Gandhara, Arachosia, Gedrosia, the Paropamisadae, and the chance to seal his gains not by bleeding the frontier dry, but by binding his rival in treaty.

i18n.hi: चंद्रगुप्त को भी बहुत कुछ तौलना था: गांधार, अराख़ोशिया, गेड्रोसिया, पेरोपामिसादाई — और यह मौका कि सीमा को खून से नहीं, एक संधि से पक्की करे, प्रतिद्वंद्वी को बाँधकर।

panels.shot: wide shot of a neutral ground between the two camps, prepared for a parley
panels.action: Between two distant camps, a cleared space near the river is marked by standards and carpets, with Indian and Greek guards standing apart as a simple wooden bench awaits the two kings.

dialogue.text: Somewhere near the Indus, between armies held in check, the kings chose a harder courage: to meet not as nameless enemies in dust and steel, but as rulers bargaining over mountains and men.

i18n.hi: कहीं सिंधु के पास, रुके हुए लश्करों के बीच, दोनों राजाओं ने कठिनतर बहादुरी चुनी: धूल–लोहा में गुमनाम दुश्मन नहीं, बल्कि पहाड़ों और इंसानों पर सौदेबाज़ी करते शासक बनकर मिलना।

panels.shot: symmetrical medium shot, low-angle, as Chandragupta and a Greek kingly figure approach each other
panels.action: Chandragupta walks forward in fine Indian court dress and armor, while opposite him a regal Greek figure in cuirass and cloak approaches; both extend their hands in formal greeting under the watchful eyes of retainers.

dialogue.text: Appian remembers only that Seleucus crossed the Indus and then, after war, made terms with Sandrocottus. The details are lost, but the outcome shines clearly through Greek and Indian memory.

i18n.hi: एपियन बस इतना याद रखता है कि सेल्युकस ने सिंधु पार किया और फिर, युद्ध के बाद, सैंड्रोकॉटस से समझौता किया। ब्योरे खो गए, पर अंजाम यूनानी और भारतीय स्मृति में साफ चमकता है।

panels.shot: close-up of Chandragupta's hand resting on a stylized map that shows the ceded satrapies
panels.action: A hand adorned with a Mauryan signet ring presses down on a parchment map where Gandhara, Arachosia, Gedrosia, and the Paropamisadae are shaded toward the Indian side, while mountains mark the new frontier.

dialogue.text: Strabo tells that Seleucus surrendered his eastern satrapies—Gandhara, Arachosia, Gedrosia and the lands of the Paropamisadae—to Chandragupta, yielding Alexander's farthest conquests to an Indian empire.

i18n.hi: स्ट्रैबो लिखता है कि सेल्युकस ने अपने पूर्वी सूबे—गांधार, अराख़ोशिया, गेड्रोसिया और पेरोपामिसादाई की धरती—चंद्रगुप्त को सौंप दिए, अलेक्ज़ांडर की दूरस्थ विजयें एक भारतीय साम्राज्य को थमा दीं।

panels.shot: medium shot of Indian and Greek envoys exchanging garlands and signet seals
panels.action: Indian and Greek emissaries stand opposite each other, one offering a garland and gold, another passing a ring and a rolled document, as attendants hold trays with ornaments and ceremonial weapons.

dialogue.text: There was also epigamia, a marriage alliance. Greek writers debate its meaning, but all agree it drew the two courts into lawful kinship, whether through a single union or broader rights of intermarriage.

i18n.hi: एक एपिगामिया भी था, विवाह-संधि। यूनानी लेखक अर्थ पर बहस करते हैं, पर सब मानते हैं कि इसने दोनों दरबारों को वैध रिश्तेदारी में बाँध दिया, चाहे एक शादी से हो या व्यापक अंतरविवाह के अधिकार से।

panels.shot: wide shot of an immense line of war elephants being led past Greek soldiers
panels.action: A seemingly endless column of colossal elephants, armored and tusked, moves in solemn file past rows of astonished Greek soldiers, their handlers guiding them with goads and quiet words.

dialogue.text: In return, Seleucus received something India had in abundance and the Hellenistic world coveted: five hundred war elephants, a living tide of muscle and discipline moving west under carved bells and chains.

i18n.hi: इसके बदले सेल्युकस को मिला वह जिसे भारत भर-भर कर दे सकता था और हेल्लेनिस्टिक दुनिया तरसती थी: पाँच सौ युद्ध हाथी, घंटियों और ज़ंजीरों से सजे, मांस–लोहे की चलता हुआ दरिया बनकर पश्चिम की ओर बढ़ते हुए।

panels.shot: medium close-up of a Greek officer's face as an elephant's massive foot passes within arm's length
panels.action: A Greek officer stares upward in awe, his helmet slightly askew, as the vast, tree-trunk leg of an elephant steps carefully beside him, dust rising softly around its padded foot.

dialogue.text: So many beasts… each a walking tower of war.

i18n.hi: इतने जानवर… हर एक खुद चलता–फिरता युद्ध-मीनार।

panels.shot: dynamic wide shot of the elephant convoy crossing a high pass out of India
panels.action: The long train of elephants winds along a narrow mountain path, clouds drifting below them, handlers cloaked against the chill as the line disappears slowly into the western valleys.

dialogue.text: Slowly, patiently, the five hundred crossed the passes. Years later, at Ipsus in 301 BCE, those Indian elephants would help decide another war, pinning Antigonus' forces and tilting power in the west.

i18n.hi: धीरे, ठहर–ठहर कर, वे पाँच सौ दर्रे पार करते गए। सालों बाद 301 ईसा पूर्व में इप्सुस पर वही भारतीय हाथी एक और जंग का फैसला मोड़ेंगे, एंटिगोनस की फौजें जकड़कर पश्चिम की ताकत का तराजू झुका देंगे।

panels.shot: medium shot of a dignified Greek ambassador approaching the gates of a grand Indian city
panels.action: A Greek nobleman in travel-worn but fine garments walks toward the monumental wooden and brick gates of a vast city, as Indian guards and curious townsfolk watch from under the shade of fig trees.

dialogue.text: With peace came another bridge. Seleucus sent Megasthenes as ambassador to Chandragupta's court, the first of the Greek observers whose reports would carry the image of India back to the Aegean.

i18n.hi: शांति के साथ एक और पुल बना। सेल्युकस ने मेगास्थनीज़ को चंद्रगुप्त के दरबार में दूत भेजा, यूनानियों में पहला जिसने भारत की छवि फिर एजियन तक पहुँचा दी।

panels.shot: majestic interior wide of Chandragupta's court at Pataliputra
panels.action: Inside a vast pillared hall with polished floors and carved wooden railings, Chandragupta sits on a raised platform as courtiers and guards stand arrayed; a lone Greek ambassador bows before the Mauryan king.

dialogue.text: In Pataliputra, Megasthenes saw the ordered streets, the timber palisades, the crowded markets and the disciplined Mauryan administration, and he set them down in Greek words as Indika.

i18n.hi: पाटलिपुत्र में मेगास्थनीज़ ने सीधी-सधी गलियाँ, लकड़ी के प्राचीर, ठसाठस बाज़ार और अनुशासित मौर्य प्रशासन देखा, और इन्हें यूनानी शब्दों में ‘इंडिका’ के नाम से दर्ज कर गया।

panels.shot: medium close-up of Chandragupta listening intently to Megasthenes, with a faint smile
panels.action: Chandragupta leans slightly forward on his throne, eyes keen and face calm, as the Greek envoy gestures respectfully, describing something with careful hands amidst a hush in the hall.

dialogue.speaker: chandragupta_maurya
dialogue.text: Let him write what he sees. The world will know our order.

i18n.hi: उसे जो दिखे, लिखने दो। दुनिया हमारी व्यवस्था जान लेगी।

panels.shot: wide shot of the rocky landscape around Kandahar with bilingual inscriptions carved into a cliff
panels.action: A rugged hillside near Kandahar shows carved inscriptions on a stone face, written in neatly cut Greek letters alongside Indian script, while a traveler pauses below to read.

dialogue.text: A generation later, under Ashoka, Greek words carved into rock at Kandahar would confirm Mauryan rule over these western lands, proof in stone of the frontier Chandragupta had fixed with Seleucus.

i18n.hi: एक पीढ़ी बाद, अशोक के समय, कंधार की चट्टानों पर खुदे यूनानी शब्द इन पश्चिमी इलाकों पर मौर्य सत्ता की गवाही देंगे, पत्थर में दर्ज वह सीमा जो चंद्रगुप्त ने सेल्युकस के साथ तय की थी।

panels.shot: serene wide shot of Chandragupta standing on a fortified hilltop, surveying a peaceful borderland
panels.action: Chandragupta stands atop a sturdy hill fort, watching traders, farmers, and small patrols move calmly along a road that winds toward distant mountains marking the horizon frontier.

dialogue.text: By ceding elephants for satrapies, and choosing treaty over endless war, Chandragupta Maurya secured the Indus frontier, turned Alexander's farthest march into a Mauryan province, and bound India and the Hellenistic world in wary, lasting connection.

i18n.hi: हाथियों के बदले सूबे छोड़कर, और अंतहीन युद्ध की जगह संधि चुनकर, चंद्रगुप्त मौर्य ने सिंधु सीमा सुरक्षित की, अलेक्ज़ांडर की आख़िरी चढ़ाई को मौर्य प्रांत में बदला और भारत व हेल्लेनिस्टिक दुनिया को सतर्क पर टिकाऊ रिश्ते में बाँध दिया।

title_i18n.hi: सेल्युकस के लिए पाँच सौ हाथी

subtitle_i18n.en: When the Indus Changed Masters and Elephants Shaped Empires
subtitle_i18n.hi: जब सिंधु बदला मालिक और हाथियों ने साम्राज्य गढ़े
